title: Colysis longissima
taxonomy: Colysis longissima is classified as a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It belongs to the genus Colysis, which is a group of ferns native to tropical regions. Colysis longissima is further categorized within the family Polypodiaceae, which comprises a large number of fern species. The classification of Colysis longissima is based on its morphological characteristics and molecular analysis. Colysis longissima is a member of the phylum Pteridophyta, which includes all ferns and fern allies.

intro: Colysis longissima, also known as Colysis fern, is a species of fern native to the tropical regions of Africa and Asia. This plant has been traditionally used for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate respiratory issues and infections. The bioactive constituents of Colysis longissima include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
